title: Q2 renewal forecast variance tightens to 1.7%
anchor: Actual ARR landed at $1,770,000 versus the $1,800,000 forecast, a $30,000 variance.
body: Actual ARR landed at $1,770,000 versus the $1,800,000 forecast, a $30,000 variance. Trailing four quarters averaged 8.2% variance.
trace: Q2 forecast accuracy significantly improved versus historical performance.

title: Mid-market NRR reaches 112% in Q2
anchor: Mid-market segment closed Q2 at 112% NRR across $1,116,000 in renewing ARR.
body: Mid-market segment closed Q2 at 112% NRR across $1,116,000 in renewing ARR. Trailing three quarters averaged 104.9%.
trace: Mid-market NRR acceleration beyond historical trend.

title: Multi-product customers expand at 124% NRR in Q2
anchor: 46 multi-product accounts delivered 124% NRR versus 102% for 74 single-product accounts.
body: 46 multi-product accounts delivered 124% NRR versus 102% for 74 single-product accounts. Multi-product NRR in Q1 was 116%.
trace: Multi-product expansion accelerated quarter over quarter while single-product held flat.

title: At-risk pool contracts to $220K across 3 accounts in April
anchor: April at-risk ARR sits at $220,000 across 3 accounts, with 2 in the top-20-ARR tier.
connect: March carried $310,000 across 7 accounts with 5 top-20 accounts.
body: April at-risk ARR sits at $220,000 across 3 accounts, with 2 in the top-20-ARR tier. March carried $310,000 across 7 accounts with 5 top-20 accounts.
trace: Four accounts dropped from at-risk status with no new additions.

title: High-touch tier delivers 96% GRR in Q2
anchor: High-touch coverage achieved 96% GRR across 22 accounts representing 18.3% of the customer base.
connect: Tech-touch coverage achieved 82% GRR across 98 accounts.
body: High-touch coverage achieved 96% GRR across 22 accounts representing 18.3% of the customer base. Tech-touch coverage achieved 82% GRR across 98 accounts.
trace: Coverage tier retention spread is 14 percentage points.

title: Mid-market time to first value improves to 23 days in Q2
anchor: Q2 mid-market new-customer cohort reached first value in 23 days across 24 accounts.
connect: Q1 mid-market cohort required 38 days across 22 accounts.
body: Q2 mid-market new-customer cohort reached first value in 23 days across 24 accounts. Q1 mid-market cohort required 38 days across 22 accounts.
trace: TTFV improvement of 15 days quarter over quarter for mid-market.

title: License utilization climbs among mid-market accounts
anchor: 22 mid-market customers exceed 80% license utilization in Q2.
body: 22 mid-market customers exceed 80% license utilization in Q2. Q1 showed 14 mid-market customers above 80%.
trace: Eight additional mid-market accounts crossed the 80% utilization threshold.

title: Mid-market health distribution shifts to 78% green in Q2
anchor: 45 of 58 mid-market accounts score green health in Q2, representing 78% of the segment.
connect: Q1 showed 71% green health across 41 of 58 accounts.
body: 45 of 58 mid-market accounts score green health in Q2, representing 78% of the segment. Q1 showed 71% green health across 41 of 58 accounts.
trace: Four additional accounts moved to green health status.

title: Atlas Insights drives expansion in 75% of mid-market adopters
anchor: 18 of 24 mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded ARR during Q2.
connect: Multi-product share of the customer base sits at 38.3%.
body: 18 of 24 mid-market customers running Atlas Insights expanded ARR during Q2. Multi-product share of the customer base sits at 38.3%.
trace: Strong correlation between Atlas Insights adoption and expansion behavior.

title: Customer health reviews generate 8 expansion opportunities in April
anchor: Health review sessions sourced $340,000 in expansion opportunities across 8 accounts.
connect: March health reviews sourced 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance.
body: Health review sessions sourced $340,000 in expansion opportunities across 8 accounts. March health reviews sourced 6 opportunities with 83% sales acceptance.
trace: Health review expansion sourcing increased by two opportunities month over month.

title: Enterprise segment NRR moderates to 106% in Q2
anchor: Enterprise renewals delivered 106% NRR across $432,000 in renewing ARR with 94% GRR.
connect: Mid-market achieved 112% NRR with 91% GRR in the same period.
body: Enterprise renewals delivered 106% NRR across $432,000 in renewing ARR with 94% GRR. Mid-market achieved 112% NRR with 91% GRR in the same period.
trace: Enterprise segment showing lower expansion rate despite higher retention.

title: 90-day retention improves to 88% for Q1 cohort
anchor: Q1 new-customer cohort shows 88% retention at 90 days across 60 accounts with 26-day TTFV.
connect: Q4 cohort achieved 83% retention across 55 accounts with 38-day TTFV.
body: Q1 new-customer cohort shows 88% retention at 90 days across 60 accounts with 26-day TTFV. Q4 cohort achieved 83% retention across 55 accounts with 38-day TTFV.
trace: Faster TTFV correlating with improved early retention.

title: TTFV under 30 days correlates with 91% renewal rate
anchor: Mid-market accounts reaching first value within 30 days renewed at 91% in Q1.
connect: Accounts with TTFV over 60 days renewed at 74%.
body: Mid-market accounts reaching first value within 30 days renewed at 91% in Q1. Accounts with TTFV over 60 days renewed at 74%.
trace: 17-point renewal rate spread based on onboarding velocity.

title: Green health accounts renewed at 95% in Q1
anchor: 39 of 41 green-health customers completed renewals in Q1 for 95% conversion.
connect: Yellow-health customers renewed at 75% with 9 of 12 converting.
body: 39 of 41 green-health customers completed renewals in Q1 for 95% conversion. Yellow-health customers renewed at 75% with 9 of 12 converting.
trace: Health score bands showing predictive value for renewal outcomes.

title: Beacon Logistics renews $280K three months early
anchor: Beacon signed renewal on April 21 for $280,000 ARR, ahead of July 15 contract end date.
connect: Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15.
body: Beacon signed renewal on April 21 for $280,000 ARR, ahead of July 15 contract end date. Custom Permissions and Audit Logs ships June 15.
trace: Early renewal timing aligns with upcoming product launch timeline.

title: Health review sourcing outperforms outbound expansion by 2x
anchor: March health reviews achieved 83% sales acceptance rate across 6 opportunities.
connect: Outbound expansion achieved 41% acceptance across 17 opportunities.
body: March health reviews achieved 83% sales acceptance rate across 6 opportunities. Outbound expansion achieved 41% acceptance across 17 opportunities.
trace: Health review methodology significantly outperforming cold outbound expansion.

title: Coverage tier retention spread narrows in Q2
anchor: High-touch and tech-touch GRR spread sits at 14 percentage points in Q2.
connect: Q1 showed an 8 percentage point spread between tiers.
body: High-touch and tech-touch GRR spread sits at 14 percentage points in Q2. Q1 showed an 8 percentage point spread between tiers.
trace: Coverage tier performance divergence widened quarter over quarter.

title: Small business segment delivers 102% NRR with 86% GRR
anchor: Small business renewals reached 102% NRR across $252,000 in renewing ARR.
connect: Enterprise achieved 106% NRR and mid-market reached 112%.
body: Small business renewals reached 102% NRR across $252,000 in renewing ARR. Enterprise achieved 106% NRR and mid-market reached 112%.
trace: Small business showing lowest expansion rate across all segments.

title: Executive sponsor depth correlates with enterprise renewal success
anchor: 4 of 9 enterprise renewals signed in April involved sponsors whose engagement deepened during Q1.
connect: 2 top-20 at-risk accounts have executive sponsor reviews scheduled this week.
body: 4 of 9 enterprise renewals signed in April involved sponsors whose engagement deepened during Q1. 2 top-20 at-risk accounts have executive sponsor reviews scheduled this week.
trace: Executive relationship investment showing correlation with renewal outcomes.
